correct_answer: There is an unclear difference in efficacy between the two drugs at these doses
reasoning: I was really unsure on this one.  The abstract was a tough read and the AI summary was very useful but it still left me uncertain.  I went with the first consideration of staying on the methadone and addressing the issues by another strategy.

highlights.text: Critically, this difference was "Not significant" (Relative Benefit Reduction 13%; 95% CI −10% to 31%). The confidence interval crosses zero, indicating no statistically proven superiority of one over the other for retention at this dose level.
buprenorphine is "less efficacious than methadone therapy, for treatment retention
The abstract does not include sedation as a measured outcome. Therefore, this specific evidence cannot be used to promise the patient that buprenorphine will be less sedating than methadone.
BMT vs MMT... did not differ for benzodiazepine use

considered are:
